WITH monthly_purchases AS (
    SELECT DISTINCT 
        user_id,
        DATE_TRUNC('month', TO_DATE(purchase_date || '-01', 'YYYY-MM-DD')) AS purchase_month
    FROM purchase
),
streak_groups AS (
    SELECT 
        user_id,
        purchase_month,
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY user_id ORDER BY purchase_month) AS row_num,
        purchase_month - INTERVAL '1 month' * ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY user_id ORDER BY purchase_month) AS streak_group
    FROM monthly_purchases
),
streak_lengths AS (
    SELECT 
        user_id,
        COUNT(*) AS streak_length
    FROM streak_groups
    GROUP BY user_id, streak_group
),
max_streaks AS (
    SELECT 
        user_id,
        MAX(streak_length) AS longest_streak
    FROM streak_lengths
    GROUP BY user_id
)
SELECT 
    longest_streak AS streak_length,
    COUNT(*) AS num_users
FROM max_streaks
GROUP BY longest_streak
ORDER BY streak_length DESC

WITH tab_with_pure_cardid AS (
    SELECT 
        cardid,
        -- Extract pure_cardid by taking part before first hyphen and part after second hyphen
        CASE 
            WHEN cardid LIKE '%-%-%' THEN
                SUBSTRING(cardid FROM 1 FOR POSITION('-' IN cardid)) || 
                SUBSTRING(cardid FROM POSITION('-' IN SUBSTRING(cardid FROM POSITION('-' IN cardid) + 1)) + POSITION('-' IN cardid))
            ELSE cardid
        END AS pure_cardid,
        type
    FROM card_info
),
min_type_per_pure_cardid AS (
    SELECT 
        pure_cardid,
        MIN(type) AS min_type
    FROM tab_with_pure_cardid
    GROUP BY pure_cardid
)
SELECT 
    t.cardid,
    t.pure_cardid,
    m.min_type
FROM tab_with_pure_cardid t
JOIN min_type_per_pure_cardid m ON t.pure_cardid = m.pure_cardid
ORDER BY t.pure_cardid, t.cardid

    a.attname AS base_col,
    (SELECT r.relname FROM pg_class r WHERE r.oid = c.conrelid) AS referencing_table,
    UNNEST((SELECT array_agg(attname) FROM pg_attribute WHERE attrelid = c.conrelid AND array[attnum] <@ c.conkey)) AS referencing_col,
    pg_get_constraintdef(c.oid) AS constraint_sql

WITH draw_groups AS (
    SELECT
        m.id,
        m.date,
        m.home_team_goal,
        m.away_team_goal,
        CASE WHEN m.home_team_goal = m.away_team_goal THEN 1 ELSE 0 END AS is_draw,
        -- Create a group identifier that increments for each draw
        SUM(CASE WHEN m.home_team_goal = m.away_team_goal THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) 
            OVER (ORDER BY m.id ROWS UNBOUNDED PRECEDING) AS draw_group
    FROM match AS m
),
accumulated_goals AS (
    SELECT
        id,
        date,
        home_team_goal,
        away_team_goal,
        is_draw,
        draw_group,
        -- Sum goals for all matches (draws and non-draws) within each group
        SUM(COALESCE(home_team_goal, 0) + COALESCE(away_team_goal, 0)) 
            OVER (PARTITION BY draw_group ORDER BY id ROWS UNBOUNDED PRECEDING) AS running_total
    FROM draw_groups
)
SELECT
    id,
    date,
    1 AS invoiced,
    running_total AS amount
FROM accumulated_goals
WHERE is_draw = 1
ORDER BY id, date

SELECT c1.user_id, COUNT(*) 
FROM user_actions c1 
WHERE c1.action = 'SEARCH'
  AND EXISTS (
    -- Check there was a LOGIN before this SEARCH
    SELECT 1 
    FROM user_actions c2 
    WHERE c2.user_id = c1.user_id 
      AND c2.action = 'LOGIN' 
      AND c2.action_time < c1.action_time
  )
  AND NOT EXISTS (
    -- Check there's no BUY between the previous LOGIN and next LOGIN
    SELECT 1 
    FROM user_actions c3 
    WHERE c3.user_id = c1.user_id 
      AND c3.action = 'BUY' 
      AND c3.action_time > (
        -- Most recent LOGIN before this SEARCH
        SELECT MAX(c4.action_time) 
        FROM user_actions c4 
        WHERE c4.user_id = c1.user_id 
          AND c4.action = 'LOGIN' 
          AND c4.action_time < c1.action_time
      )
      AND c3.action_time < COALESCE((
        -- Next LOGIN after this SEARCH (if any)
        SELECT MIN(c5.action_time) 
        FROM user_actions c5 
        WHERE c5.user_id = c1.user_id 
          AND c5.action = 'LOGIN' 
          AND c5.action_time > c1.action_time
      ), '9999-12-31'::date)
  )
  AND NOT EXISTS (
    -- Ensure this SEARCH happens before the next LOGIN
    SELECT 1 
    FROM user_actions c6 
    WHERE c6.user_id = c1.user_id 
      AND c6.action = 'LOGIN' 
      AND c6.action_time < c1.action_time
  )
GROUP BY c1.user_id

SELECT

    COUNT(*) as matches,
    SUM(CASE 
        WHEN (m.home_team_api_id = t.team_api_id AND m.home_team_goal > m.away_team_goal) OR
             (m.away_team_api_id = t.team_api_id AND m.away_team_goal > m.home_team_goal) 
        THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) as victories,
    SUM(CASE 
        WHEN (m.home_team_api_id = t.team_api_id AND m.home_team_goal < m.away_team_goal) OR
             (m.away_team_api_id = t.team_api_id AND m.away_team_goal < m.home_team_goal) 
        THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) as defeats,
    SUM(CASE 
        WHEN m.home_team_goal = m.away_team_goal 
        THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) as draws,
    SUM(CASE 
        WHEN (m.home_team_api_id = t.team_api_id AND m.home_team_goal > m.away_team_goal) OR
             (m.away_team_api_id = t.team_api_id AND m.away_team_goal > m.home_team_goal)

        WHEN m.home_team_goal = m.away_team_goal 
        THEN 1
        ELSE 0 END) as score
FROM team t
JOIN match m ON t.team_api_id = m.home_team_api_id OR t.team_api_id = m.away_team_api_id

WITH RECURSIVE path_cte AS (
  -- Base case: Find starting cards (cards that no other card points to as nextCardId)
  SELECT id, nextCardId, id::TEXT AS Path
  FROM collection c1
  WHERE NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM collection c2 WHERE c2.nextCardId = c1.id)
  
  UNION ALL
  
  -- Recursive case: Follow the chain forward
  SELECT collection.id, collection.nextCardId, cte.Path || ' --> ' || collection.id::TEXT
  FROM collection
  JOIN path_cte cte ON collection.id = cte.nextCardId
)
-- Select only the final paths in each chain
SELECT Path
FROM path_cte c
WHERE NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM path_cte c2 WHERE c2.Path LIKE c.Path || ' --> %')
ORDER BY id

WITH qualified_posts AS (
    -- Get all posts with score >= 20
    SELECT id, acceptedanswerid, posttypeid, score, 'direct' as inclusion_type
    FROM posts 
    WHERE score >= 20
),
parent_posts AS (
    -- Get parent posts (those that are referenced by acceptedanswerid)
    SELECT p1.id, p1.acceptedanswerid, p1.posttypeid, p1.score
    FROM posts p1
    WHERE p1.id IN (SELECT acceptedanswerid FROM posts WHERE acceptedanswerid IS NOT NULL)
),
final_selection AS (
    -- Include parents with score >= 20
    SELECT id, acceptedanswerid, posttypeid, score
    FROM qualified_posts
    WHERE id NOT IN (SELECT id FROM posts WHERE acceptedanswerid IS NOT NULL)
    
    UNION
    
    -- For parent posts with score < 20, include their children with score >= 20
    SELECT p2.id, p2.acceptedanswerid, p2.posttypeid, p2.score
    FROM posts p1
    JOIN posts p2 ON p1.id = p2.acceptedanswerid
    WHERE p1.score < 20 AND p2.score >= 20
    
    UNION
    
    -- Include posts with score >= 20 that are not children of any post
    SELECT id, acceptedanswerid, posttypeid, score
    FROM qualified_posts
    WHERE id NOT IN (SELECT COALESCE(acceptedanswerid, 0) FROM posts)
)
SELECT DISTINCT id, acceptedanswerid, posttypeid, score
FROM final_selection
ORDER BY id

WITH RECURSIVE atom_ancestors AS (
    -- Base case: start with the given atom's parent
    SELECT atom_id, parent_id, enabled
    FROM connection 
    WHERE atom_id = 'TR000_4' AND parent_id IS NOT NULL
    
    UNION ALL
    
    -- Recursive case: find parents of parents
    SELECT c.atom_id, c.parent_id, c.enabled
    FROM connection c
    INNER JOIN atom_ancestors aa ON c.atom_id = aa.parent_id
    WHERE c.parent_id IS NOT NULL
),
-- Check if all ancestors are enabled
validation AS (
    SELECT COUNT(*) as total_ancestors,
           SUM(CASE WHEN enabled = TRUE THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) as enabled_ancestors
    FROM atom_ancestors
)
SELECT aa.*
FROM atom_ancestors aa
CROSS JOIN validation v
WHERE v.total_ancestors = v.enabled_ancestors OR v.total_ancestors = 0

WITH RECURSIVE 
path_a(id, parent_id, level) AS (
    -- Start with Superhero A (assuming id=1001)
    SELECT id, parent_id, 0 FROM lineage WHERE id = 1001

    -- Traverse up the lineage
    SELECT l.id, l.parent_id, pa.level + 1 
    FROM lineage l 
    INNER JOIN path_a pa ON l.id = pa.parent_id
    WHERE pa.parent_id IS NOT NULL
),
path_b(id, parent_id, level) AS (
    -- Start with Superhero B (assuming id=1201)
    SELECT id, parent_id, 0 FROM lineage WHERE id = 1201
    UNION ALL
    -- Traverse up the lineage
    SELECT l.id, l.parent_id, pb.level + 1 
    FROM lineage l 
    INNER JOIN path_b pb ON l.id = pb.parent_id
    WHERE pb.parent_id IS NOT NULL
),
ancestors_a AS (
    SELECT id FROM path_a
    UNION 
    SELECT 1001  -- Include the starting superhero itself
),
ancestors_b AS (
    SELECT id FROM path_b
    UNION 
    SELECT 1201  -- Include the starting superhero itself
)
SELECT a.id as common_ancestor_id
FROM ancestors_a a
INNER JOIN ancestors_b b ON a.id = b.id
LIMIT 1

WITH RECURSIVE EmpHierarchy AS (
    -- Base case: all employees with their direct managers
    SELECT 
        e.id as employee_id,
        e.salary as employee_salary,
        e.bossid as manager_id,
        1 as level
    FROM staff e
    WHERE e.bossid IS NOT NULL
    
    UNION ALL
    
    -- Recursive case: traverse up the hierarchy
    SELECT 
        eh.employee_id,
        eh.employee_salary,
        m.bossid as manager_id,
        eh.level + 1 as level
    FROM EmpHierarchy eh
    INNER JOIN staff m ON eh.manager_id = m.id
    WHERE m.bossid IS NOT NULL
),
QualifiedManagers AS (
    -- Find managers who earn at least twice as much as their subordinates
    SELECT 
        eh.employee_id,
        eh.employee_salary,
        eh.manager_id as boss_id,
        m.salary as boss_salary,
        eh.level
    FROM EmpHierarchy eh
    INNER JOIN staff m ON eh.manager_id = m.id

WITH RECURSIVE subgroup_connections AS (
    -- Base case: get all unique subgroup values
    SELECT DISTINCT subgroup1 as subgroup FROM superhero_group WHERE subgroup1 IS NOT NULL
    UNION
    SELECT DISTINCT subgroup2 as subgroup FROM superhero_group WHERE subgroup2 IS NOT NULL
),
connected_components AS (
    -- Start with each subgroup as its own component
    SELECT 
        subgroup,
        subgroup as component_root,
        0 as level
    FROM subgroup_connections
    
    UNION ALL
    
    -- Find connections through shared items
    SELECT 
        sc.subgroup,
        CASE 
            WHEN sc.subgroup < MIN(cc.component_root) THEN sc.subgroup 
            ELSE MIN(cc.component_root) 
        END as component_root,
        cc.level + 1
    FROM (
        SELECT sg.subgroup1 as subgroup, sg.id as item
        FROM superhero_group sg 
        WHERE sg.subgroup1 IS NOT NULL
        
        UNION ALL
        
        SELECT sg.subgroup2 as subgroup, sg.id as item
        FROM superhero_group sg 
        WHERE sg.subgroup2 IS NOT NULL
    ) sc
    JOIN (
        SELECT sg.id as item, sg.subgroup1 as subgroup
        FROM superhero_group sg 
        WHERE sg.subgroup1 IS NOT NULL
        
        UNION ALL
        
        SELECT sg.id as item, sg.subgroup2 as subgroup
        FROM superhero_group sg 
        WHERE sg.subgroup2 IS NOT NULL
    ) other_sc ON sc.item = other_sc.item
    JOIN connected_components cc ON other_sc.subgroup = cc.subgroup
    WHERE cc.level < 10  -- Prevent infinite recursion
    GROUP BY sc.subgroup
),
final_groups AS (
    SELECT 
        id as item,
        subgroup1,
        subgroup2,
        MIN(component_id) as group_id
    FROM (
        SELECT 
            sg.id,
            sg.subgroup1,
            sg.subgroup2,
            DENSE_RANK() OVER (ORDER BY COALESCE(sg.subgroup1, sg.subgroup2)) as component_id
        FROM superhero_group sg
        WHERE sg.subgroup1 IS NOT NULL OR sg.subgroup2 IS NOT NULL
    ) base_groups
    GROUP BY id, subgroup1, subgroup2
)
SELECT 
    item,
    subgroup1,
    subgroup2,
    group_id
FROM final_groups
ORDER BY item

WITH LastTransactionPerStation AS (
  -- Get the last transaction for each customer-gasstation combination
  SELECT *
  FROM (
    SELECT *,
           ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY customerid, gasstationid ORDER BY date DESC, time DESC) as rn
    FROM transactions_1k
  ) t
  WHERE rn = 1
),
FirstIssueStation AS (
  -- Find the first gas station where each customer had amount < 10
  SELECT customerid,
         FIRST_VALUE(gasstationid) OVER (PARTITION BY customerid ORDER BY date ASC, time ASC) as first_issue_gasstation
  FROM transactions_1k
  WHERE amount < 10
),
CustomerHasIssues AS (
  -- Identify which customers have any transactions with amount < 10
  SELECT DISTINCT customerid
  FROM transactions_1k
  WHERE amount < 10
),
LastValidStation AS (
  -- For customers without issues, find their last valid transaction station
  SELECT ltps.customerid,
         FIRST_VALUE(ltps.gasstationid) OVER (PARTITION BY ltps.customerid ORDER BY ltps.date DESC, ltps.time DESC) as last_valid_gasstation
  FROM LastTransactionPerStation ltps
  LEFT JOIN CustomerHasIssues chi ON ltps.customerid = chi.customerid
  WHERE chi.customerid IS NULL AND ltps.amount >= 10
)
SELECT 
  ltps.customerid,
  ltps.transactionid,
  ltps.gasstationid,
  ltps.amount
FROM LastTransactionPerStation ltps
JOIN FirstIssueStation fis ON ltps.customerid = fis.customerid
WHERE ltps.gasstationid = fis.first_issue_gasstation

UNION ALL

SELECT 
  ltps.customerid,
  ltps.transactionid,
  ltps.gasstationid,
  ltps.amount
FROM LastTransactionPerStation ltps
JOIN LastValidStation lvs ON ltps.customerid = lvs.customerid
LEFT JOIN CustomerHasIssues chi ON ltps.customerid = chi.customerid
WHERE chi.customerid IS NULL 
  AND ltps.gasstationid = lvs.last_valid_gasstation
  AND ltps.amount >= 10

CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION update_team_attributes_eff_to() 
RETURNS TRIGGER AS $$
BEGIN 
    -- Update eff_to for all records of the same team
    UPDATE team_attributes 
    SET eff_to = subquery.next_date 
    FROM (
        SELECT 
            id,
            COALESCE(
                LEAD(TO_DATE(date, 'YYYY-MM-DD HH24:MI:SS')::DATE, 1) 
                OVER (
                    PARTITION BY team_api_id 
                    ORDER BY TO_DATE(date, 'YYYY-MM-DD HH24:MI:SS')::DATE
                ),
                TO_DATE('5999-12-31', 'YYYY-MM-DD')
            ) AS next_date
        FROM team_attributes 
        WHERE team_api_id = NEW.team_api_id
    ) AS subquery
    WHERE team_attributes.id = subquery.id;
    
    RETURN NULL; 
END; 
$$ LANGUAGE plpgsql;

CREATE OR REPLACE TRIGGER after_insert_team_attributes 
AFTER INSERT ON team_attributes 
FOR EACH ROW 
EXECUTE PROCEDURE update_team_attributes_eff_to()

INSERT INTO hero_attribute_bifurcation (hero_id, attribute_id, attribute_chunk, is_bifurcated)
WITH bifurcated_data AS (
    SELECT 
        hero_id,
        attribute_id,
        attribute_value,
        CASE 
            WHEN attribute_value > 50 THEN 1 
            ELSE 0 
        END AS is_bifurcated,
        -- Calculate number of complete chunks of 50
        attribute_value / 50 AS full_chunks,
        -- Calculate remainder
        attribute_value % 50 AS remainder
    FROM hero_attribute
),
expanded_rows AS (
    -- Generate rows for each chunk of 50
    SELECT 
        hero_id,
        attribute_id,
        50 AS attribute_chunk,
        is_bifurcated
    FROM bifurcated_data
    CROSS JOIN generate_series(1, full_chunks)
    
    UNION ALL
    
    -- Add remainder row if it exists
    SELECT 
        hero_id,
        attribute_id,
        remainder AS attribute_chunk,
        is_bifurcated
    FROM bifurcated_data
    WHERE remainder > 0
    
    UNION ALL
    
    -- Handle values <= 50 (no splitting needed)
    SELECT 
        hero_id,
        attribute_id,
        attribute_value AS attribute_chunk,
        0 AS is_bifurcated
    FROM bifurcated_data
    WHERE attribute_value <= 50
)
SELECT hero_id, attribute_id, attribute_chunk, is_bifurcated
FROM expanded_rows

WITH cte_funding_count AS (
    SELECT fundingtype, COUNT(*) AS count 
    FROM schools 
    GROUP BY fundingtype
),
cte_with_percentage AS (
    SELECT 
        fundingtype, 
        count,
        count * 100.0 / (SELECT SUM(count) FROM cte_funding_count) AS exact_percent,
        ROUND(count * 100.0 / (SELECT SUM(count) FROM cte_funding_count), 0) AS rounded_percent,
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY (count * 100.0 / (SELECT SUM(count) FROM cte_funding_count)) - FLOOR(count * 100.0 / (SELECT SUM(count) FROM cte_funding_count)) DESC) AS rn
    FROM cte_funding_count
),
cte_total_check AS (
    SELECT SUM(rounded_percent) AS total_percent FROM cte_with_percentage
)
SELECT 
    fundingtype,
    count,
    CASE 
        WHEN rn <= (100 - (SELECT total_percent FROM cte_total_check))
        THEN rounded_percent + 1
        ELSE rounded_percent
    END AS percent
FROM cte_with_percentage
ORDER BY count DESC

WHERE start_time >= NOW() - INTERVAL '3 days' 
  AND end_time < NOW() + INTERVAL '5 hours'

  CASE

    ELSE 2                          -- Future events second
  END,
  start_time

WITH event_dates AS (
    SELECT DISTINCT 
        e.event_name,
        DATE(e.event_date AT TIME ZONE 'UTC' AT TIME ZONE 'MST') AS event_day
    FROM attendance a
    JOIN event e ON a.link_to_event = e.event_id
    WHERE e.event_name = 'April Meeting'
),
streak_groups AS (
    SELECT 
        event_name,
        event_day,
        event_day - INTERVAL '1 day' * ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY event_day) AS streak_group
    FROM event_dates
),
streak_counts AS (
    SELECT 
        event_name,
        MIN(event_day) AS start_date,
        MAX(event_day) AS end_date,
        COUNT(*) AS streak_count,
        MAX(event_day) AS max_end_date
    FROM streak_groups
    GROUP BY event_name, streak_group
)
SELECT 
    streak_count,
    event_name,
    'MST' AS timezone,
    start_date,
    end_date
FROM streak_counts
ORDER BY max_end_date DESC
LIMIT 1

WITH RECURSIVE cte AS (
    -- Base case: first row
    SELECT 
        id,
        amount,
        amount as cum_sum,
        1 as bucket
    FROM transfer 
    WHERE id = 1
    
    UNION ALL
    
    -- Recursive case: subsequent rows
    SELECT 
        t.id,
        t.amount,
        CASE 
            WHEN c.cum_sum + t.amount > 20 THEN t.amount 
            ELSE c.cum_sum + t.amount 
        END as cum_sum,
        CASE 
            WHEN c.cum_sum + t.amount > 20 THEN c.bucket + 1 
            ELSE c.bucket 
        END as bucket
    FROM transfer t
    JOIN cte c ON t.id = c.id + 1
)
SELECT id, amount, cum_sum, bucket
FROM cte
ORDER BY id

SELECT
    r.superhero_id AS "superheroId",
    p.power_name   AS "power",
    COUNT(*)       AS "totalRequests",
    SUM(
       CASE WHEN r.status = 'success' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
    ) * 100.0 / COUNT(*) AS "successRate"
FROM (
    SELECT
        superhero_id,
        superpower_id,
        status,
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (
            PARTITION BY main_request_uuid
            ORDER BY retry_number DESC, id DESC
        ) AS row_number
    FROM public.superhero_requests
    WHERE created_at >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '30 days'
      AND created_at <= CURRENT_DATE
) r
JOIN public.superpower p ON r.superpower_id = p.id
WHERE r.row_number = 1
GROUP BY
    r.superhero_id,
    p.power_name
LIMIT 10

WITH RECURSIVE calculated_results AS (
    -- Base case: first row
    SELECT 
        id,
        trx,
        event,
        (COALESCE(0, 0) + trx) * event AS result_good
    FROM sales_transactions 
    WHERE id = (SELECT MIN(id) FROM sales_transactions)
    
    UNION ALL
    
    -- Recursive case: subsequent rows
    SELECT 
        st.id,
        st.trx,
        st.event,
        (cr.result_good + st.trx) * st.event AS result_good
    FROM sales_transactions st
    JOIN calculated_results cr ON st.id = (
        SELECT MIN(id) 
        FROM sales_transactions 
        WHERE id > cr.id
    )
)
SELECT 
    id, 
    ROUND(result_good::numeric, 6) AS result
FROM calculated_results
ORDER BY id

WITH player_changes AS (
    SELECT 
        entity_id,
        updated_at,
        MAX(CASE WHEN column_id = 'height' THEN value::text::integer END) AS height_change,
        MAX(CASE WHEN column_id = 'weight' THEN value::text::integer END) AS weight_change
    FROM player_stats_changes
    GROUP BY entity_id, updated_at
),
cumulative_values AS (
    SELECT 
        entity_id,
        updated_at,
        height_change,
        weight_change,
        LAST_VALUE(height_change) OVER (
            PARTITION BY entity_id 
            ORDER BY updated_at 
            RANGE BETWEEN UNBOUNDED PRECEDING AND CURRENT ROW
        ) AS height_cumulative,
        LAST_VALUE(weight_change) OVER (
            PARTITION BY entity_id 
            ORDER BY updated_at 
            RANGE BETWEEN UNBOUNDED PRECEDING AND CURRENT ROW
        ) AS weight_cumulative
    FROM player_changes
)
SELECT 
    entity_id,
    COALESCE(height_cumulative, 
        FIRST_VALUE(height_cumulative) OVER (
            PARTITION BY entity_id 
            ORDER BY CASE WHEN height_cumulative IS NOT NULL THEN 0 ELSE 1 END, updated_at
        )
    ) AS height,
    COALESCE(weight_cumulative, 
        FIRST_VALUE(weight_cumulative) OVER (
            PARTITION BY entity_id 
            ORDER BY CASE WHEN weight_cumulative IS NOT NULL THEN 0 ELSE 1 END, updated_at
        )
    ) AS weight,
    updated_at
FROM cumulative_values
ORDER BY entity_id, updated_at

WITH RECURSIVE leaf_nodes AS (
    -- Get all leaf nodes (nodes that have no children)
    SELECT id, parent_id, color
    FROM superhero2 s
    WHERE NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM superhero2 s2 WHERE s2.parent_id = s.id)
      AND color IS NOT NULL
),
tree_traversal AS (
    -- Start with leaf nodes
    SELECT id, parent_id, color, 0 as level
    FROM leaf_nodes
    
    UNION ALL
    
    -- Recursively process parents
    SELECT p.id, p.parent_id, 
           CASE 
               -- Get all children of this parent that have been processed
               WHEN COUNT(c.color) = 1 THEN MAX(c.color)  -- One child
               WHEN COUNT(DISTINCT c.color) = 1 THEN MAX(c.color)  -- All children same color
               ELSE 'gray'  -- Children have different colors
           END as color,
           c.level + 1 as level
    FROM superhero2 p
    INNER JOIN tree_traversal c ON p.id = c.parent_id
    GROUP BY p.id, p.parent_id, c.level
)
UPDATE superhero2 
SET color = tt.color
FROM tree_traversal tt 
WHERE superhero2.id = tt.id

FROM (
    SELECT 
        customerid,
        key_name,
        jsonb_agg(DISTINCT value) AS unique_values
    FROM (
        SELECT 
            customerid,
            key AS key_name,
            CASE 
                WHEN jsonb_typeof(value) = 'array' THEN jsonb_array_elements_text(value)
                ELSE value::text
            END AS value
        FROM (
            SELECT 
                customerid,
                (jsonb_each(preference)).*
            FROM card_preference
            WHERE customerid = '123.abc'
        ) AS extracted_keys
    ) AS flattened_values
    GROUP BY customerid, key_name
) AS grouped_values
GROUP BY customerid

SELECT 
    u.row_id AS id,
    u.utm_campaign,
    u.utm_source,
    u.utm_medium,
    u.utm_content,
    u.utm_term,
    r.date_of_visit,
    SUM(r.sessions) AS sessions,
    SUM(r.pageviews) AS pageviews,
    SUM(r.bounces) AS bounces
FROM utm u
JOIN report r ON u.row_id = r.id
    AND (u.utm_campaign = r.utm_campaign OR (u.utm_campaign IS NULL AND r.utm_campaign IS NULL))
    AND (u.utm_source = r.utm_source OR (u.utm_source IS NULL AND r.utm_source IS NULL))
    AND (u.utm_medium = r.utm_medium OR (u.utm_medium IS NULL AND r.utm_medium IS NULL))
    AND (u.utm_content = r.utm_content OR (u.utm_content IS NULL AND r.utm_content IS NULL) OR (u.utm_content IS NULL AND r.utm_content IS NULL))
    AND (u.utm_term = r.utm_term OR (u.utm_term IS NULL AND r.utm_term IS NULL) OR (u.utm_term IS NULL AND r.utm_term IS NULL))
GROUP BY 
    u.row_id,
    u.utm_campaign,
    u.utm_source,
    u.utm_medium,
    u.utm_content,
    u.utm_term,
    r.date_of_visit
ORDER BY 
    u.row_id,
    r.date_of_visit

WITH RECURSIVE hierarchy AS (
    -- Base case: root nodes (no parent)
    SELECT
        uuid,
        card_name,
        parent_uuid,
        0 as level,
        ARRAY[uuid] as path
    FROM card_type
    WHERE parent_uuid IS NULL
    
    UNION ALL
    
    -- Recursive case: child nodes
    SELECT
        ct.uuid,
        ct.card_name,
        ct.parent_uuid,
        h.level + 1,
        h.path || ct.uuid
    FROM card_type ct
    JOIN hierarchy h ON ct.parent_uuid = h.uuid
),
-- Get direct children for each parent
parent_with_children AS (
    SELECT 
        p.uuid as parent_uuid,
        p.card_name as parent_name,
        ARRAY_AGG(c.uuid) as child_uuids,
        ARRAY_AGG(c.card_name) as child_names
    FROM card_type p
    LEFT JOIN card_type c ON c.parent_uuid = p.uuid
    WHERE p.parent_uuid IS NOT NULL OR EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM card_type WHERE parent_uuid = p.uuid)
    GROUP BY p.uuid, p.card_name
)
SELECT 
    ct.uuid,
    ct.card_name,
    pwc.child_uuids,
    pwc.child_names
FROM card_type ct
LEFT JOIN parent_with_children pwc ON ct.uuid = pwc.parent_uuid
WHERE ct.parent_uuid IS NOT NULL OR EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM card_type WHERE parent_uuid = ct.uuid)
ORDER BY ct.card_name

),
prices_in_window AS (

    FROM card_prices
    WHERE uuid = '5f8287b1-5bb6-5f4c-ad17-316a40d5bb0c'
      AND timestamp >= '2022-07-12T15:30:00-00:00'

WITH RECURSIVE craft_costs AS (
    -- Base case: direct items with their prices
    SELECT 
        s.item_id,
        s.item_price,
        s.quantity,
        s.uses_item_id,
        s.uses_item_price,
        s.resource_id,
        s.resource_price,
        0 as level
    FROM store s
    
    UNION ALL
    
    -- Recursive case: find components of components
    SELECT 
        cc.item_id,
        cc.item_price,
        cc.quantity * s.quantity as quantity,
        s.uses_item_id,
        s.uses_item_price,
        s.resource_id,
        s.resource_price,
        cc.level + 1
    FROM craft_costs cc
    JOIN store s ON cc.uses_item_id = s.item_id
    WHERE cc.level < 10  -- Prevent infinite recursion
),
partial_craft AS (
    SELECT 
        item_id,
        item_price as retail_price,
        SUM(COALESCE(resource_price * quantity, 0)) as partial_craft_price
    FROM store
    GROUP BY item_id, item_price
),
full_craft AS (
    SELECT 
        item_id,
        item_price as retail_price,
        SUM(COALESCE(uses_item_price * quantity, 0)) + SUM(COALESCE(resource_price * quantity, 0)) as full_craft_price
    FROM craft_costs
    GROUP BY item_id, item_price
)
SELECT 
    p.item_id,
    p.retail_price,
    p.partial_craft_price,
    f.full_craft_price,
    CASE 
        WHEN p.retail_price <= LEAST(p.partial_craft_price, f.full_craft_price) THEN 'Buy'
        WHEN p.partial_craft_price <= f.full_craft_price THEN 'Partial Craft'
        ELSE 'Full Craft'
    END as best_option
FROM partial_craft p
JOIN full_craft f ON p.item_id = f.item_id
ORDER BY p.item_id

CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION f_gap_fill_update(tbl text, id_col text, order_col text) 
RETURNS void LANGUAGE plpgsql AS $func$
DECLARE 
    tmp text[];
    col text;
    sql_stmt text;
BEGIN
    -- Get all nullable columns for the specified table
    SELECT array_agg(column_name) 
    INTO tmp
    FROM information_schema.columns 
    WHERE table_name = tbl 
    AND is_nullable = 'YES'
    AND column_name NOT IN (id_col, order_col);
    
    -- Loop through each nullable column
    FOREACH col IN ARRAY tmp LOOP
        -- Build the UPDATE statement with LAG window function for forward fill
        sql_stmt := 'UPDATE ' || tbl || ' t1 SET ' || col || ' = t2.ff_' || col || 
                   ' FROM (SELECT ' || id_col || ', ' || order_col || ', ' || col || 
                   ', COALESCE(' || col || ', LAG(' || col || ') OVER (PARTITION BY ' || id_col || ' ORDER BY ' || order_col || ')) AS ff_' || col || 
                   ' FROM ' || tbl || ') t2 ' ||
                   'WHERE t1.' || id_col || ' = t2.' || id_col || 
                   ' AND t1.' || order_col || ' = t2.' || order_col || 
                   ' AND t1.' || col || ' IS DISTINCT FROM t2.ff_' || col;
        
        -- Execute the dynamic SQL
        EXECUTE sql_stmt;
    END LOOP;
END;
$func$

WITH card_activity AS (
    SELECT 
        to_date(fe.start_date_key::text, 'YYYYMMDD') AS start_date,
        to_date(fe.end_date_key::text, 'YYYYMMDD') AS end_date,
        dp.set_name,
        dp.set_code
    FROM fact_collection fe
    INNER JOIN dim_set dp ON fe.set_key = dp.set_key
),
months AS (
    SELECT generate_series(
        date_trunc('month', (SELECT MIN(start_date) FROM card_activity)),
        date_trunc('month', (SELECT MAX(COALESCE(end_date, CURRENT_DATE)) FROM card_activity)),
        INTERVAL '1 month'
    ) AS month_series
)
SELECT 
    m.month_series AS month,
    ca.set_name,
    COUNT(*) AS existing_cards
FROM months m
CROSS JOIN (SELECT DISTINCT set_name FROM card_activity) sets
LEFT JOIN card_activity ca ON (
    ca.set_name = sets.set_name
    AND ca.start_date < m.month_series + INTERVAL '1 month' 
    AND (ca.end_date IS NULL OR ca.end_date >= m.month_series)
)
WHERE ca.set_name IS NOT NULL
GROUP BY m.month_series, ca.set_name
ORDER BY m.month_series, ca.set_name

CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION reindex_superhero_hair_colours(IN publisher_id_param BIGINT) 
RETURNS VOID 
LANGUAGE plpgsql 
AS $$
DECLARE
    hero_record RECORD;
    counter INTEGER := 1;
BEGIN
    -- Loop through superheroes for this publisher and renumber hair_colour_id sequentially
    FOR hero_record IN 
        SELECT id 
        FROM superhero 
        WHERE publisher_id = publisher_id_param 
        ORDER BY id
    LOOP
        UPDATE superhero 
        SET hair_colour_id = counter 
        WHERE id = hero_record.id;
        counter := counter + 1;
    END LOOP;
END;
$$

WITH RECURSIVE user_groups AS (
    -- First, create pairs of users that should be grouped together
    SELECT DISTINCT 
        u1.id as user1_id, 
        u2.id as user2_id
    FROM users u1
    JOIN users u2 ON u1.ref1 = u2.ref1 OR u1.ref1 = u2.ref2 OR u1.ref2 = u2.ref1 OR u1.ref2 = u2.ref2
    WHERE u1.id <= u2.id
    
    UNION
    
    SELECT DISTINCT 
        u1.id as user1_id, 
        u2.id as user2_id
    FROM users u1
    JOIN users u2 ON u1.ref1 = u2.ref1 OR u1.ref1 = u2.ref2 OR u1.ref2 = u2.ref1 OR u1.ref2 = u2.ref2
    WHERE u1.id > u2.id
),
connected_components AS (
    -- Find all connected components (groups of related users)
    SELECT 
        user1_id as user_id,
        MIN(user1_id) OVER (PARTITION BY CASE 
            WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM user_groups ug2 WHERE ug2.user1_id = user_groups.user1_id AND ug2.user2_id = user_groups.user2_id) 
            THEN user_groups.user1_id 
            ELSE user_groups.user2_id 
        END) as group_id
    FROM user_groups
),
user_to_group AS (
    -- Map each user to their group
    SELECT DISTINCT user_id, group_id
    FROM connected_components
),
group_info AS (
    -- Get info for each group
    SELECT 
        utg.group_id,
        ARRAY_AGG(DISTINCT u.id) AS ids,
        ARRAY_AGG(DISTINCT u.username) AS usernames,
        ARRAY_AGG(DISTINCT u.ref1) FILTER (WHERE u.ref1 IS NOT NULL) AS refs1,
        ARRAY_AGG(DISTINCT u.ref2) FILTER (WHERE u.ref2 IS NOT NULL) AS refs2
    FROM user_to_group utg
    JOIN users u ON utg.user_id = u.id
    GROUP BY utg.group_id
),
owners_to_check AS (
    -- Get all distinct owners from assets
    SELECT DISTINCT owner FROM assets
),
group_assets AS (
    -- Count assets per group
    SELECT 
        gi.group_id,
        gi.ids,
        gi.usernames,
        gi.refs1,
        gi.refs2,
        COUNT(a.id) AS asset_count
    FROM group_info gi
    LEFT JOIN users u ON u.id = ANY(gi.ids)
    LEFT JOIN assets a ON a.owner = u.ref1 OR a.owner = u.ref2
    GROUP BY gi.group_id, gi.ids, gi.usernames, gi.refs1, gi.refs2
),
unmatched_assets AS (
    -- Handle assets whose owners aren't in users table
    SELECT 
        a.owner,
        COUNT(a.id) as asset_count
    FROM assets a
    WHERE NOT EXISTS (
        SELECT 1 FROM users u WHERE a.owner = u.ref1 OR a.owner = u.ref2
    )
    GROUP BY a.owner
)
SELECT 
    ids,
    usernames,
    refs1,
    refs2,
    asset_count
FROM group_assets
WHERE asset_count > 0

UNION ALL

SELECT 
    NULL::integer[] as ids,
    NULL::text[] as usernames,
    NULL::text[] as refs1,
    ARRAY_AGG(owner) as refs2,
    SUM(asset_count) as asset_count
FROM unmatched_assets
ORDER BY asset_count DESC
